name: requests
description: Use the requests library to send GET requests to an API and parse JSON responses.
example: Use the requests library to fetch https://api.example.com/data and extract the 'items' field.

name: httpx
description: Use httpx's asynchronous client to make API calls.
example: Use httpx.AsyncClient to asynchronously fetch a news API and parse headlines.

name: pandas
description: Use pandas to read CSV files and perform data aggregation.
example: Load the sales.csv file and compute total sales grouped by region.

name: polars
description: Use polars to efficiently read and filter large datasets.
example: Read a large CSV file with polars and select rows where price > 100.

name: flask
description: Use Flask to build a simple web service with a form.
example: Create a web page with a form where users enter their name and receive a greeting.

name: sanic
description: Use Sanic to implement asynchronous web APIs.
example: Build an asynchronous JSON API that returns the current server time.

name: pydantic
description: Use pydantic to define and validate data models.
example: Create a User model and validate that input JSON matches the required schema.

name: msgpack
description: Use msgpack to serialize and deserialize data into binary format.
example: Serialize a Python dictionary into bytes using msgpack and then deserialize it.

name: scrapy
description: Use Scrapy to build a web crawler to scrape website content.
example: Crawl example.com and extract all article titles.

name: selectolax
description: Use selectolax to quickly parse HTML documents.
example: Extract all hyperlink URLs from an HTML page using selectolax.

name: click
description: Use click to create CLI tools with argument parsing.
example: Build a CLI tool that accepts a --name argument and prints a greeting.

name: typer
description: Use typer to build CLI apps with type hints.
example: Create a CLI app that takes two integers and prints their sum.

name: asyncio
description: Use asyncio to run asynchronous tasks concurrently.
example: Fetch multiple websites in parallel and print response times.

name: trio
description: Use trio to write structured asynchronous programs.
example: Launch three async tasks in parallel with trio, each waiting a random number of seconds.

name: axios
description: Use axios to make HTTP requests and handle JSON responses.
example: Send a GET request to https://api.example.com/data using axios and extract the title field.

name: got
description: Use got as a lightweight and powerful alternative to axios for HTTP requests.
example: Fetch JSON from a remote API using got and print selected fields.

name: express
description: Use Express to build web APIs and serve HTTP endpoints.
example: Create a simple Express server with a route that returns a JSON greeting.

name: koa
description: Use Koa to build lightweight, async-first web servers.
example: Create a Koa server that returns the current time in JSON format.

name: mongoose
description: Use mongoose to interact with MongoDB using schema-based models.
example: Define a User schema with mongoose and save a new user to MongoDB.

name: sequelize
description: Use Sequelize to define SQL models and perform database operations.
example: Define a Product model with Sequelize and query products priced over $100.

name: yup
description: Use yup to define and validate object schemas.
example: Create a yup schema to validate user input with email and password fields.

name: zod
description: Use zod to declare and parse structured data with validation.
example: Define a zod schema for a signup form and validate incoming payloads.

name: commander
description: Use commander to build CLI applications with argument support.
example: Create a CLI tool with commander that takes a filename and prints it.

name: inquirer
description: Use inquirer to create interactive command-line interfaces.
example: Build a CLI wizard that prompts the user to select a template and enter a name.

name: rxjs
description: Use RxJS to handle event streams and asynchronous data flows.
example: Create an observable that emits values every second and logs them.

name: socket.io
description: Use socket.io to create a real-time chat server and client.
example: Build a WebSocket server with socket.io that broadcasts messages to all clients.

name: axios
description: Use axios with typed responses for safe HTTP requests.
example: Send a GET request to https://api.example.com/items and type the response as an array of Item.

name: zod
description: Use zod to validate and infer TypeScript types from schemas.
example: Create a Zod schema for a user form and use it to infer the form's TypeScript type.

name: class-validator
description: Use class-validator to add validation decorators to TypeScript classes.
example: Define a CreateUserDTO class with @IsEmail and @Length validators and validate an input object.

name: io-ts
description: Use io-ts to decode and validate external data while preserving static types.
example: Define an io-ts codec for a BlogPost and parse JSON data from an API.

name: tsyringe
description: Use tsyringe to manage dependency injection with decorators.
example: Use @injectable and @inject decorators to register and resolve service dependencies.

name: typedi
description: Use typedi for lightweight dependency injection in service-based architectures.
example: Inject a LoggerService into a UserService using typedi and resolve it from the container.

name: type-graphql
description: Use type-graphql to build GraphQL APIs using classes and decorators.
example: Define a GraphQL resolver with TypeScript decorators and schema auto-generation.

name: nestjs
description: Use NestJS to build modular, scalable TypeScript server-side applications.
example: Create a REST controller in NestJS that handles user registration with validation and dependency injection.

name: tsoa
description: Use tsoa to automatically generate OpenAPI docs from TypeScript decorators.
example: Create a controller with tsoa and generate OpenAPI documentation from routes and models.

name: ts-pattern
description: Use ts-pattern to perform exhaustive pattern matching in a type-safe way.
example: Match an API response variant with ts-pattern and handle all possible cases safely.

name: fp-ts
description: Use fp-ts to write functional programs using Option, Either, and Task data types.
example: Use Either to handle success/failure in parsing user input and chaining validation logic.

name: gin
description: Use Gin to build fast and lightweight HTTP web servers.
example: Create a RESTful API with Gin that returns a list of users in JSON format.

name: fiber
description: Use Fiber, an Express-inspired framework, to create fast web applications in Go.
example: Build a simple web server with Fiber and implement a route to return current server time.

name: gorilla/mux
description: Use Gorilla Mux for advanced HTTP routing in Go applications.
example: Set up URL path parameters and handle different HTTP methods with Gorilla Mux.

name: go-resty/resty
description: Use Resty as a rich HTTP client for sending requests and handling responses.
example: Send a POST request with JSON data and handle the response using Resty.

name: go-chi/chi
description: Use Chi for building REST APIs with a minimal and idiomatic router.
example: Implement middleware and nested routes using Chi in a RESTful service.

name: gorm
description: Use GORM to interact with relational databases through Go structs.
example: Define a model struct and use GORM to perform CRUD operations on a PostgreSQL database.

name: ent
description: Use Ent to generate type-safe, statically-typed Go code for database interactions.
example: Define an Ent schema and use generated code to create and query users.

name: cobra
description: Use Cobra to build powerful CLI applications with subcommands.
example: Create a CLI app with Cobra that supports `init`, `build`, and `deploy` commands.

name: urfave/cli
description: Use urfave/cli for quick CLI app creation with flags, arguments, and commands.
example: Build a CLI tool with flags to process files and print results.

name: go-co-op/gocron
description: Use gocron to schedule periodic jobs like cron in Go.
example: Schedule a background task to run every 10 minutes using gocron.

name: asynkron/protoactor-go
description: Use Proto.Actor to write concurrent and distributed actor-based systems in Go.
example: Create a set of actors that communicate via messages and manage task state.

name: go-kit/kit
description: Use Go Kit to build distributed services with logging, transport, and metrics.
example: Implement a microservice endpoint with transport and middleware using Go Kit.

name: actix-web
description: Use Actix-web to build fast and secure web servers with the Actix actor system.
example: Create a RESTful API with Actix-web that responds with a JSON list of products.

name: rocket
description: Use Rocket for easy-to-use, type-safe web frameworks in Rust with built-in security.
example: Set up a simple web server with Rocket that returns a greeting message.

name: hyper
description: Use Hyper for low-level HTTP implementations in both client and server contexts.
example: Build an HTTP server with Hyper that handles incoming GET requests.

name: reqwest
description: Use Reqwest to send HTTP requests and handle responses in an easy, type-safe manner.
example: Send an asynchronous GET request with Reqwest and parse the JSON response.

name: diesel
description: Use Diesel for safe, extensible ORM in Rust to interact with SQL databases.
example: Define a schema with Diesel and perform CRUD operations on a PostgreSQL database.

name: sqlx
description: Use SQLx for asynchronous SQL queries with compile-time checked queries.
example: Perform asynchronous database operations with SQLx and handle results with futures.

name: tokio
description: Use Tokio as an asynchronous runtime for building scalable applications.
example: Create an async server using Tokio that handles requests concurrently.

name: async-std
description: Use async-std for async programming in Rust with a focus on simplicity.
example: Build an asynchronous TCP server using async-std and handle client connections.

name: serde
description: Use Serde to serialize and deserialize Rust data structures efficiently.
example: Serialize a struct to JSON and deserialize it back into Rust objects using Serde.

name: tide
description: Use Tide for simple, asynchronous web applications with a focus on ease of use.
example: Create a basic HTTP server with Tide that handles POST requests with JSON bodies.

name: futures
description: Use Futures for handling asynchronous computations and combinators.
example: Use the Futures library to chain multiple asynchronous operations together.

name: tokio-tungstenite
description: Use tokio-tungstenite to work with WebSocket connections in asynchronous Rust applications.
example: Build an echo server that sends received WebSocket messages back to clients using tokio-tungstenite.
